title: Atropa belladonna (Deadly Nightshade)
intro: Atropa belladonna (Deadly Nightshade) has been used for centuries for medicinal purposes due to its alkaloids - especially Scopolamine. It's commonly used as an antiemetic, antispasmodic, and muscle relaxant. The primary components are Atropine and Hyoscyamine which act on the central nervous system and gastrointestinal tract. Preparations include tinctures, extracts, and powders. However, side effects can be severe including constipation, dizziness, dry mouth, confusion, and even seizures or coma if ingested in large quantities. Therefore, it's crucial to follow precise dosages under medical supervision, especially for children and pregnant women.
